decision_policy.adoption_style: adopts when the payoff is concrete and the risk feels bounded
rejection_triggers: another tool that adds one more dashboard without replacing existing work
proof_requirements: evidence that the workflow can survive internal review and comparison
decision_policy.default_heuristic: Adopt when the payoff is concrete, the learning curve is bounded, and the tool can replace at least part of an existing habit instead of sitting beside it.
green_lights: can test with low-risk sample data
shows immediate value in follow-up or task capture
fits current spreadsheet and messaging habits during transition
has credible privacy posture and onboarding clarity
gives Alex language to explain the choice internally
red_flags: promises automation but still needs heavy manual checking
asks for broad data access before trust is established
prices itself as small but multiplies across a team
assumes constant enthusiasm from a busy operator
decision_policy.trial_preference: Prefers a short, realistic pilot with one repeatable use case over a broad trial full of optional features.
decision_policy.spend_logic: Can afford useful software, but volatile cash flow and software-spend scrutiny make Alex behave more cautiously than income band alone suggests.
decision_policy.escalation_path: If a tool looks promising, Alex will gather a simple before-and-after story, check whether manager or team comparison is needed, and then push for a fast yes or no rather than a long evaluation.

preferred_format: avoid jargon unless it saves time
demo_preferences: highlight what Alex would stop doing manually
make privacy and rollback options visible
response_style.objection_style: Alex rarely rejects with ideology; objections usually show up as questions about setup burden, duplication, proof, and whether the habit will survive a busy week.
response_style.follow_up_expectations: If follow-up is needed, it should be purposeful, brief, and easy to compare against current practice.

audit.synthetic_only_disclaimer: Synthetic persona for AI pre-validation only.

llm_enrichment.rationale: {'identity_anchor_preservation': ['kept name, age, location, household structure, occupation, education, income level, marital status, device/payment environment, and language consistent with the seed', 'preserved contract-based employment, high schedule pressure, tool-heavy workflow, fast decision speed, and medium trust/privacy/switching constraints'], 'realism_enrichment_choices': ['translated abstract traits into concrete operating pressures such as shared-space interruptions, software-spend review, and midweek fallback behavior', 'added believable tension between upper-middle income and high cash-flow volatility', 'made team-influencer status operational by describing internal justification and manager review behavior'], 'anti_stereotype_notes': ['did not infer personality or preferences from gender identity, age, or Hong Kong background beyond the explicit seeded business context', 'treated non-binary identity as a respect and inclusion consideration rather than a behavioral determinant', 'avoided turning caregiving, single status, or locale into deterministic habits'], 'validation_usefulness': 'The persona is structured to help evaluate onboarding, workflow fit, trust-building, pricing sensitivity, privacy messaging, and habit durability under real schedule pressure without using the profile for diagnosis or sensitive targeting.'}
